pattern: Since every candidate coordinate's total signal only depends on integer distances to a handful of towers, sweeping a small integer grid and scoring each cell directly turns an apparent optimization problem into a plain exhaustive search with a fixed tie-break rule.
hints: Nothing requires you to search near the towers only -- since the total quality at a coordinate only depends on distances that are cheap to compute, you can safely brute-force every candidate coordinate on a small integer grid and score it directly.

For a fixed candidate coordinate, sum the floor-divided contribution of every tower whose real Euclidean distance to that coordinate is at most radius, and keep a running best total as you sweep the grid.

When you find a new total that's strictly greater than your current best, replace it -- but never replace it on a tie. If you sweep x ascending and, within each x, y ascending, always keeping the first-found best on ties guarantees the lexicographically smallest coordinate wins by construction.

edge_inputs.description: zero-quality tower at the origin with the minimum radius -- every reachable cell scores 0, so the lexicographically smallest coordinate wins
max-value tower at the far grid corner with the max radius -- best coordinate is the tower's own location
two equally strong towers placed symmetrically, forcing a real tie in total quality that must break lexicographically
three towers stacked on the exact same coordinate, so their qualities must all add up at that single point
two far-apart towers with a small radius -- neither can influence the other's neighborhood, so the search must scan the whole grid, not just cluster near one tower

stress.description: random towers (count, coordinates, and qualities all randomized) with a random radius, sized up to the maximum tower count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(50, max(1, (size % 50) + 1))
    towers = [[rng.randint(0, 50), rng.randint(0, 50), rng.randint(0, 50)] for _ in range(n)]
    radius = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [towers, radius]

stress.description: a second random spread with a different seed to vary tower density and radius
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(50, max(1, (size % 50) + 1))
    towers = [[rng.randint(0, 50), rng.randint(0, 50), rng.randint(0, 50)] for _ in range(n)]
    radius = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [towers, radius]
